title: Sharing dashboards without sharing connected boards
excerpt:
Hi everyone! 
We wanted to share that this feature is currently planned for H2 2025 for ENT customers. At the moment, we don’t have a timeline for releasing this functionality for non-Portfolio users, but we’re actively …

posts.author: Jeannot (Jeannot Damoiseaux)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

We wanted to share that this feature is currently planned for H2 2025 for ENT customers. At the moment, we don’t have a timeline for releasing this functionality for non-Portfolio users, but we’re actively exploring how we can make this available more broadly.

Thanks for your patience and for sharing your feedback - it’s crucial in helping us shape the platform! We will keep you updated as we progress.

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:
This seems like a great idea that my organization could use as well. We have a Board of Directors who are sometimes in flux, and we want them to have an overview of what’s going on, but we don’t always want all of them to be able to see the details.

Guest access to certain dashboards could also be a useful way to expand our communication with donors - we could let them see where their money is going and make them feel more involved, without also giving them access to messy behind-the-scenes work, client contact info, etc.

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a big deal for us as we’d like to aggregate a dashboard for our clients where they could interact (comment on updates, leave an update on an item, etc) but without needing access to the specific board those items come from.

posts.author: mitchellr3 (Mitchell Rubinstein)
posts.cookedHtml: This is huge. We have the exact same issue. I suppose you can grant access to the underlying boards and then restrict access to column info but that’s a huge pain. Even within our organization I need to share aggregated data on a dashboard without anyone able to see underlying detail (think anonymous employee surveys). I believe I’ve put in a feature request for this, but reiterating this would be something that is extremely useful.

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml: Exactly! You basically run into a dead end if you don’t want them to see your behind the scenes workflows (i.e. specific groups or items).

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have put in a feature request as well. My experience has been Monday.com priorities what people want. I’m simply trying to corral everyone who wants to see dashboard or guest view functionalities updated in one place!

With specific use cases. For us, it’s client view/interaction as a service based company (which is funny because you’d think Monday.com would go hmmm a lot of users want to share Monday.com with guests + introduce them to the software #Win#Win!!!

For AJ (above) it’s non-profit/dashboard visibility which I think is huge! I would love that if the 501C3s we donated to had an interactive dashboard…

posts.author: M.kell (Meredith Keller)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please! This feature is essential, even within our own company, never mind external guests.

posts.author: Viewport (Julius Jeppe)
posts.cookedHtml: Sharing dashboards without sharing connected boards would be amazing. Sharing in fullscreen / TV mode only seems like a good step…

posts.author: emetzner13 (Evan Metzner)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be a great feature for us as well. This is pretty essential for us, so we might have to leave the platform if this doesn’t get implemented in some way unfortunately.

posts.author: PM-Jeremy (Jeremy)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update to this. I cannot figure out a way to share certain information with customers unless I keep MANUALLY duplicating information making it really difficult and time consuming to manage the different projects we have an the go. I have tried printing to PDF, but that never comes out right. I tried “printing” dash board but that does not work. All I want to do is send them the Gantt chart without sharing ALL of my information and other customer information.

posts.author: Pelle (Pelledegraaf)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this voted idea? It is a real shame we can’t share our dashboards with guests.

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml:
Context/problem

Currently, when you view a dashboard, the charts and graphs will only reflect data from connected boards that you have access to. Meaning, if you don’t have permission to the boards that supply the data to the dashboard, you can’t view the graphs and charts in the dashboard. I do like this - because of the drill-down features of the graphs and charts, it makes sense that dashboard viewers need permission in order to be able to view that data. However, there are many cases where you might want to share the graphs and charts with another user, without giving them access to the data that generates them.

For example, I might want to have a graph that shows this months sales, and I might want to share the graph with people outside of my team so they can see how sales are progressing through the week, but I might not want those people to be able to drill down and see the details of each individual sale.

Or for another example, I might want to have a workload widget that shows my available capacity, and I might want to share this with people from other teams that regularly require my services, but I might not want those people to be able to drill down to see the details of all of my tasks.

Currently, there is no way to do this in Monday.com (as far as I can tell).

Possible solution(s)

If the drill-down features are the only reason that users should not be able to view graphs and charts on a dashboard without permission to the connected boards, then being able to disable drill-down features could solve the problem. (If there are other reasons, then I’d love to hear them.)

  1. Perhaps you could have a dashboard setting called “report mode” that disables all drill-down features, so users can view the graphs and charts without being able to drill down into the data that generates them.
  2. Or, even better, enable owners to invite users to the dashboard with a new permission level called “report viewers” that disables all drill-down features for that specific user, so they can view graphs and charts without being able to drill down in the data that generates them, while other users of the same dashboard who are invited with normal permissions can use the dashboard with full functionality.

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml:
Since posting this it seems things have changed slightly. A user is no longer able to view a dashboard if they haven’t got access to ALL of the boards connected to the dashboard. This is definitely a very important improvement as previously there was a risk that people would be misled by the graphs and charts they were seeing since it would have had an incomplete set of data if the user didn’t have permission to all of the boards. However, this change has no impact on my desire to share dashboards with colleagues without having to give them access to the connected boards.

My preferred solution would be that when a user is added to a dashboard that is connected to boards they do not have access to, I’m notified the same way I currently am about which boards they do not have permissions for, but instead of being completely blocked out of the dashboard the user would simply see the graphs and charts without the ability to drill-down into the boards and items that power them.

posts.author: MDubYa (Matt Weiszbrod)
posts.cookedHtml: We have been hoping to create a global dashboard that has multiple boards organized in one time line to send out to our executives and partners. The main issue is that we don’t want them to need a login, we just want them to be able to view the time line/calendar. Sharing this dashboard would be inc…

posts.author: MJ-AIFS (MJ)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are interested in doing a similar thing. We need to share a view only view of a dashboard or embed a view anywhere so that executives can review up to date project statuses without being Monday.con users.
Thanks
MJ

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Pelle @MJ-AIFS

While waiting for Monday’s Product team to implement this, you can use an add-on such as Analytics & Reports by Screenful . You can use it to create custom charts with your Monday data and compile them into Reports . The reports can then be shared with anyone in your organization via email or Slack as a PDF on a custom schedule.

Here’s a guide for creating reports like the one above.

You can configure everything once, and your manager will start receiving weekly project status reports that are automatically generated with the most up-to-date data.

You can learn more about this add-on, or start a free trial to give it a try with your own data.

posts.author: GJohnston (Garrett Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be interested in being able to share a dashboard with our managers as well without the need to give them access to all the connected boards. The ability to still search and view would be perfect with no need for them to use specific drill downs to see the rest of the data outside of the filters applied to the dashboard. The reason for being that all data for all stores is available and they would only need to see their stores which I have broken down into multiple dashboards

posts.author: Han (Kuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com team

it will be mega delightful to know if the request is moving to the sprint plans of function add-up, please. In my perspective, the user would like to have the dashboard “Read Only” for the guests account or any senior teammate.

The connected board is great for admin or project managers’ data maintenance ONLY

posts.author: ivangarcia (Ivan Garcia _Appfire_)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

If you want to share specific dashboards with external stakeholders – and you’re up for trying an app – you should check out Dashboard Hub. It is developed by Appfire, company I work for.

Dashboard Hub is an app that empowers anyone in the team to easily create and share powerful dashboards with formulas, custom charts and nearly 100 metrics – regardless of technical skills.

It allows to create secure and unique public links to share dashboards and reports to external stakeholders in seconds. As you can see in the GIF below, people with the link (and the password) will only see that specific dashboard, without compromising any data from other dashboards.

Why don’t you try it out 14 days for free?

Iván García, Product Marketing Manager

posts.author: mmerchat (Monica Merchat)
posts.cookedHtml: Isn’t this already working? Don’t dashboard users see the full aggregated content from boards they have permission to see but only those?

posts.author: AdamCPU (Adam Raphael)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d suggest this could be allowed by having dashboards embedable with drill down features disabled, so that they can be shared with stakeholders who don’t have a Monday account.

posts.author: ltufts (LeighAnn Tufts)
posts.cookedHtml: I desperately need this feature. What’s the latest, Monday.com team? @Julietteb

posts.author: sharonkwok (Sharon Kwok)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this? This is really a business crucial feature we’re missing right now.

posts.author: emount (Evan)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would also be great if it could be applied to the viewer role as well. We want to share dashboards with internal teams; however, the connected boards have information for other teams as well that we don’t want to be visible. Currently, we are having to set the connected boards’ permissions to only allow viewers to view items they are assigned and assigning the members from each team to all of the items/subitems that we want summarized in the dashboard.

posts.author: jhamb (Josh Beck)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding my 2 cents here. My company needs to share live, high-level project info with our clients but it is disadvantageous for them to have access to the overall information. This really only needs to be a view-only document (and it’d be great if they didn’t have to login to Monday to view it.)

Monday team, is there any movement on getting this put on the road map?

posts.author: LosonszkiS (Silvia Losonszki)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great idea! It’s a huge limitation in our case as well.
Would be great, if accesses to dashboards wouldn’t be dependent on accesses to the connected boards.

posts.author: coach34 (Rich)
posts.cookedHtml: My company just purchased the enterprise of monday and this inability is a huge issue. I echo all the votes that Monday needs to REDESIGN the adminstration of Monday so that BOARD owners (NOT ACCOUNT OWNERS) can control security permissions, access (by function) so we can give the best experience to our users. Right now our corporate office owns the account so as a board user we run risk of data issues having everyone edit the data when all they need is VIEW ability.

posts.author: vanceism7 (Vance Palacio)
posts.cookedHtml: It is quite frankly ridiculous that dashboards can’t be shared without also sharing the underlying board data. Its a super basic requirement that one should be able to share a sort of presentation view without giving viewers access to see all of the underlying details. Monday.com seems to have no interest in fixing this seeing as this issue has been going on for years without a single response from a monday rep. Very disappointing. I keep running into disappointments with Monday…

posts.author: amy.douglas (Amy Douglas)
posts.cookedHtml: We desperately needs this as well and so many accounts are asking for this. I love the idea of a toggle switch that prevents drilling into the underlaying dashboards.

posts.author: dorth (Dana)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to echo that this would be a game-changer in user experience and sharing project status dashboards with clients. We haven’t done it yet, but I’ve envisioned workarounds of creating a specific board per client to mirror the data to allow outside guests to only see THAT information. The levels of this would include our high level portfolio board with team boards which include our projects as items, then mirroring specific projects to another board to maybe invite a guest to so they can come and see what is on a dashboard (beyond a PDF export or screenshare).
But even internally, if we can allow view access to a dashboard without going through levels of account to board permissions to then realize they can’t see certain information, etc., this would be great!

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wouldn’t this be solved by the advanced permissions on Enterprise plans?

You can set users so that they are only able to see and edit items that are assigned to them on the Board.

posts.author: KatRJ (Katrina)
posts.cookedHtml:
Until this urgently needed feature is added, Monday needs to change the text embedded in the automatic emails that go out when inviting a guest or viewer to a board. Currently that email says “If you are not part of 's Monday.com account, you’ll join as a free, read-only, viewer to the account.”

As this is false, it should be removed from the template.

posts.author: srogers (Susan Rogers)
posts.cookedHtml: I will add our desire to have this feature…we have the need to share portions of a board so that the business units can see status of their requests for work, and ONLY their requests. Also since this is status - they should not be able to edit anything, nor should they be able to change the filters or the columns shown. We need a way to present the status information - live and pulling from the source board – but with “view only” permissions.

posts.author: kwallack1 (Kenneth Wallace)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this feature as well. We have suppliers who view items in our boards but we do not want to share the connected boards because it would be too much of a hassle to update the contacts across all boards.

posts.author: eranggg (eran)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need this feature aswell.
This is the reason i didnt move to monday for now because i cant find a solution for that.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community, I wanted to let you know that we spoke with our product about creating sharable dashboards without connected boards and the status of this feature is currently under consideration. This means and while we do not have any plans to develop this feature at the moment, we are considering adding it to our roadmaps in the future. When thinking about the upcoming roadmap, our team needs to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize new features, improve old ones, and ensure overall platform quality.

Every quarter we take the feature request with the most votes and discuss them with our Product team.

To learn more about the feature request process, how it works, and how to become part of the magic read here.

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this feature.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi community, I just wanted to upload that this feature is now under development and will hopefully be released in Q4. I will share more about this feature and its capabilities as the team gets ready to start testing and releasing it. Thank you for your feedback and helping us bring these features to life!

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Very happy to hear this!

At our org we are anxiously awaiting the more robust permissions model that is coming, but the ability to report read-only to dashboards without board membership will help mitigate our other permissions needs.

posts.author: Lblando (Luke Blando)
posts.cookedHtml: Thats fantastic news! For our use case, we need to be able to use the calendar feature in dashboards and share a live calendar with our clients. This will be huge.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julietteb Any update on this feature as we kick off Q4?

posts.author: LaShaye (LaShaye Floyd)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to know if there is update about guest dashboard sharing as well. I really don’t want our executive team or board members seeing every single detail. It would just cause confusion. Please keep us posted so we know what to expect. We’re looking for comparable solutions around this topic as we approach 2025. Thank you!

posts.author: jamief (Jamie Fitzwater)
posts.cookedHtml: Echoing what others are saying—would be great if this actually came out in Q4 of this year. I’ve spent a bazillion hours trying to build a workaround that would work for us, but nothing does. Dashboard Hub came close but is super glitchy and constantly crashing. Pleaseeeee fix this.

posts.author: ashleyabright (Ashley)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julietteb — is there any update on this ability? Maybe I am missing how to use it. Anyone know?

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes @Julietteb and @ashleyabright I need an update also! I’ve been holding off our company all year saying new permissions options are coming. We were led to believe we could be confident it would be released this calendar year. I sure hope this is not delayed past the end of the year.

posts.author: ashleyabright (Ashley)
posts.cookedHtml: @hbristol ditto! I have an agency and this sharing ability is so needed to keep clients up-to-date. @Julietteb, it seems the Q4 projected launch is passing. Is there a new timeline so we can adjust our hopes/expectations? Thank you so much!

posts.author: LaShaye (LaShaye Floyd)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy New Year 2025 Everyone! Just checking in as well to see if any progress has been made in this area regarding dashboard sharing without sharing connected boards.

posts.author: LaShaye (LaShaye Floyd)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Ashley. I’m waiting for a solution as well!

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! We wanted to share that this feature is currently in Beta for Portfolio users. At the moment, we don’t have a timeline for releasing this functionality for non-Portfolio users, but we’re actively exploring how we can make this available more broadly.

Thanks for your patience and for sharing your feedback - it’s crucial in helping us shape the platform! We will keep you updated as we progress.

posts.author: JackHolway (Jack)
posts.cookedHtml: Also looking for an update, can someone from Monday please share an updated timeline for this?

posts.author: JackHolway (Jack)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Amy, is there a resource for Portfolio users that walks through how to use this feature/participate in the beta?

posts.author: tim.sheaffer1 (Tim Sheaffer)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb - Do you have any updates on this feature?

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @amybronstein could you elaborate on “Portfolio users”? Does this mean for the Portfolio boards only? And not for regular Dashboards?

posts.author: Krunchy (Guillaume Servonnat)
posts.cookedHtml:
It looks like we were promised the moon but in the end, only the enterprise tier will get that feature,
Unfortunately, It’s becoming a frequent & bad habit of Monday not to completely address the need of their users. Either the new feature is not complete (reorder views, formulas in automations…), either the feature is for a special tier, usually the most expensive one.
Frustrating…

posts.author: hculver (Hannah)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great to have this feature, but it’s very unfortunate that it seems they’re only interested in rolling it out to those who are paying for their highest tier. It would be great to have this feature available for anyone.

posts.author: nkern (Nick Kern)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this been implemented?

posts.author: TDGregory (Tabetha)
posts.cookedHtml: Where is this feature located to be turned on? I checked in labs and I don’t see it. I am an enterprise admin with portfolios.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @amybronstein @Julietteb any update or clarification on this feature?
